title: Squalris

{{Quote|Slick as a Squalris.|A common saying among traders along the Perlemian Trade Route, referring to a hard bargainer|<ref name="GATORW" />}}
'''Squalrises''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Color/Legends|yellow]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ifmix VI]] in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region. The Squalris anatomy was characterized by a corpulent frame, bristly hair garnishing the face, and a rounded crest running the length of the [[Bone/Legends|skull]]. The quest for fortune and social standing was the driving factor in the Squalris psyche. This led Squalrises to become formidable businessbeings and dealmakers. The species had a natural proclivity for charisma and sociability, which aided such deals. They tended to prefer ostentatious attire that marked the wearer's wealth and status.

Ifmix VI became part of the [[galactic community]] in the earliest days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and the blazing of the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] in the region allowed Squalris [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] to spread beyond the [[Ifmix system/Legends|Ifmix system]] to ply their trade. One such merchant was [[Sanglui]], who opened an import–export business on [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. With the outbreak of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Sanglui joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and secretly used his prominent position to aid the Rebellion against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Squalris_side_view.jpg|thumb|left|upright|A narrow crest ran the length of the Squalris skull.]]
Squalrises were heavy-set [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who averaged 1.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. They had thick, [[Color/Legends|yellow]] skin that gave them some resistance to injury.<ref name="GATORW" /> They had five fingers per [[Limb/Legends|hand]], and each of these had a hard fingernail on the back.<ref name="CSWTEA 3" /> A fringe of thick, coarse, yellow hairs grew from the Squalris chin and jowls,<ref name="GATORW" /> framing the broad face.<ref name="CSWE III 185" /> The mouth was broad, with thick lips stretching just below the small, flat nose. Two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]—yellow with either a yellow<ref name="GATORW" /> or [[red]] iris<ref name="CSWTEA 3" />—were set deep within a bony brow, nude in some members of the [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="GATORW" /> but trimmed with [[black]] eyebrows in others. A narrow crest ran from just behind the eyes to the back of the head along the top of the [[Bone/Legends|skull]].<ref name="CSWTEA 3" /> A floppy ear fringed in bristly hairs hung from both sides of the head.<ref name="GATORW" />

Squalrises entered adolescence at eleven [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard years]] of age, entering maturity at nineteen [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]]. Middle age began at age fifty-five. Squalris life expectancy was ninety standard years.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Society and culture==
Typically, Squalrises were gregarious gadabouts, known for their easy-going and generally non-violent demeanor. As charismatic beings with a gift for getting their way in negotiations, Squalrises were unrepentant capitalists who loved to haggle and dicker over business transactions. The species was so renowned for their hard-nosed bargaining that [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] along the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] used the phrase "slick as a Squalris" to characterize a deft dealmaker. In fact, the surest way to nettle a Squalris was to threaten his or her business interests. This acumen made the average Squalris wealthier than his or her counterparts among other species, which was only in keeping with the general Squalris desire for success, money, property, and social status. Members of the species advertised their riches by bedecking themselves in foppish clothing: boots and jewelry, as well as luxurious tunics, trousers, overcoats, and robes, all bedizened with elaborate embroidery and gemstones.<ref name="GATORW" />

The Squalrises claimed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ifmix VI]] as their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Their native [[Language/Legends|language]] was also known as [[Squalris (language)|Squalris]], which had both spoken and written forms. Most members of the species also learned to read and write [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Many Squalris names had only a single part, such as Feemus, Irallish, Klabelon, Muaago, and Sanglui. Others had two parts, such as Zlorr Uluj.<ref name="GATORW" />

==History==
The Squalrises evolved on Ifmix VI, a planet known for its unforgiving weather: turbulent winds and [[tornado]]es ravaged the surface in an unending cycle.<ref name="GATORW" /><ref name="CSWE II 78">{{CSWECite|II|78|Ifmix VI}}</ref> The Squalrises joined the [[galactic community]] soon after the formation of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]; outsiders reached their world sometime between [[27,500 BBY|27,500]] and c.[[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]. After the invention of advanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] in 25,000 BBY, Ifmix VI became part of the newly blazed Perlemian Trade Route, situated between the worlds [[Yabol Opa/Legends|Yabol Opa]] toward the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] and [[Shulstine V]] toward the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Rim]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Squalris merchants soon became common in [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] and trading posts, especially those lying along the Perlemian.<ref name="GATORW" /> The [[Ifmix system/Legends|Ifmix system]] became part of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region. The Squalrises' homeworld remained in an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the Galactic Republic through a litany of conflicts, ranging from the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] of [[3996 BBY]] to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].> By [[137 ABY]], the Squalrises' home [[Star system/Legends|system]] fell within territory controlled by [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Squalrises in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Our contact is extremely high in the government of this planet… And he's our only hope of diverting the incredible wealth of Vorzyd 5 away from the Empire, and back to the people! We must keep his identity secret at any risk!|Sanglui, to Leia Organa, Paxin, and Luke Skywalker|<ref name="CSWTEA 3" />}}
[[File:Rebel_cell_on_Vorzyd_V.jpg|thumb|right|In 1 ABY, Sanglui met with Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker, and Paxin to plan their liaison with a powerful government contact.]]
Travelers along the Perlemian frequently encountered Squalrises, particularly Squalris [[Noble/Legends|nobles]] and [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]] making their way as merchants. These members of the species were often preoccupied with enhancing their personal prestige through success in business and trade.<ref name="GATORW" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], at least one Squalris supported the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. This individual, named [[Sanglui]], operated publicly as the well-heeled proprietor of an import–export company, headquartered in opulent offices atop a [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscraper]] in the [[High Town]] area of [[Efavan/Legends|Efavan]] on the planet [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]]. Using his cover as a vivacious but innocuous socialite in the upper echelons of Vorzyd V society, Sanglui hobnobbed with the rich and powerful, all the while working to undermine the Imperial presence on the world. Sanglui headed the Rebel cell in Efavan.<ref name="GATORW" /> In this capacity, he received a secret communication via [[Courier droid/Legends|message droid]] from a [[Natala Vanden|Rebel contact]] embedded deep in the government of Vorzyd V. Sanglui was tasked with arranging a meeting with the contact and one of the Rebels on the world as part of a plan to sever the Empire's access to Vorzyd V's coffers—earned from the [[Casino/Legends|casinos]] that earned it the nickname "[[Gambling/Legends|Gambler's]] World." After Sanglui drew straws with fellow rebels [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Paxin]], and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to see who would meet the contact, Skywalker won.<ref name="CSWTEA 3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Artist and writer [[Russ Manning]] designed the Squalrises and introduced them in the ''[[Gambler's World]]'' series of [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strips]] published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] from [[March 12]] to [[September 8]], [[1979]]. The story features the Squalris Sanglui, although the story never states the name of his species. The portion of the story featuring Sanglui was reprinted by [[Dark Horse Comics]] as [[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures 3|''Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures'' 3]] in [[1994]]. For this re-issue, [[Ray Murtaugh]] colorized the old strips, showing the Squalrises as yellow-skinned beings for the first time. ''[[Gambler's World]]'' has since been republished as part of the [[Star Wars comic books|trade paperback]] [[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures (trade paperback)|''Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures'']] and as a [[Star Wars comic books|webstrip]] on the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']] website.

In [[2004]], [[Wizards of the Coast]] published the book ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. The volume includes a detailed look at the Squalrises, revealing the species' name, homeworld, temperament, and other details. It includes rules to allow players of [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|''The Star Wars Roleplaying Game'']] to portray Squalris characters, statistics that make Squalrises less agile than [[Human/Legends|Humans]] but hardier and more likable. Members of the species also receive a bonus to resist damage due to their thick skin, to diplomacy, to detecting people's hidden agendas, and to starting wealth. The book was co-written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Jason Fry]], [[Jeff Quick]], [[Jeffrey Quinn]], and [[Daniel Wallace]]. The book includes an image of a Squalris drawn by [[Mark Tedin]].<ref name="GATORW" />

There has been some slight confusion as to the site of Ifmix VI, the Squalris homeworld. According to ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', it is located near the Perlemian Trade Route.<ref name="CSWE II 78" /> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', however, shows it lying ''on'' the Perlemian. This article follows the latter source, as it was published more recently.<ref name="TEA" />
